# Blog Post Template

> Three variant structures for different content types. Pick the one that fits, then delete the others. Lead each major section with the conclusion in the first 30-50 words, then provide context and evidence (answer-first pattern).

---

## Variant A — How-To

For practical, step-based content. Best for the Practitioner persona.

```yaml
---
brief-type: blog-post
variant: how-to
target-persona: practitioner
tone-variant: clear-practical
readability-target: grade-9
---
```

### Prerequisites

[What the reader needs before starting. Be specific — versions, tools, access.]

### Step 1 — Installing [thing]

[Answer-first: what this step accomplishes. Then the instructions.]

### Step 2 — Configuring [thing]

[Answer-first: what this step accomplishes. Then the instructions.]

### Step 3 — Running [thing]

[Answer-first: what this step accomplishes. Then the instructions.]

### Conclusion

[What the reader can now do. One actionable next step.]

---

## Variant B — Analysis

For evidence-based takes on industry patterns. Best for the Technical Leader persona.

```yaml
---
brief-type: blog-post
variant: analysis
target-persona: technical-leader
tone-variant: conversational-specific
readability-target: grade-10
---
```

### The Situation

[What's happening, grounded in specific data. No "the industry is changing" — say what changed, with numbers.]

### What We Found

[Your analysis or research findings. Separate observation from interpretation.]

### What It Means

[Implications for the reader's work. Connect to their JTBD.]

### What to Do

[Concrete next steps. Not "consider adopting" — name the action.]

---

## Variant C — Case from the Field

For sharing engagement learnings. Best for the Honest Executive persona.

```yaml
---
brief-type: blog-post
variant: case-from-the-field
target-persona: honest-executive
tone-variant: thoughtful-grounded
readability-target: grade-9
---
```

### What Happened

[The situation — 2-3 sentences. Anonymize if needed, but be specific: "a 200-person fintech" not "a leading organization."]

### What We Did

[Actions taken, tools used, timeline. Be concrete.]

### What Worked

[Results, with data where possible. Don't overclaim.]

### What Was Hard

[Friction, trade-offs, surprises. This section builds credibility.]

---

## Content Hub Topics

Align your post with one of three hub topics:

| Topic | Register | Focus |
|-------|----------|-------|
| AI Literacy | Educational, grounded | Making AI adoption legible to teams and leaders |
| Agentic Workflows | Technical, practical | How to build and govern agent-based systems |
| Governance Patterns | Analytical, evidence-based | Patterns, anti-patterns, and lessons from real engagements |

## The Depth Test

Before publishing: would a senior engineering leader find this useful? If it only tells them what they already know, go deeper or don't publish. See [Content Guidelines — The Depth Test](../ux/content-guidelines.md#the-depth-test).

---
